title: Wire DeckSlotPolicy.Validate into deck generation and archetype ranking
description:
After DeckSlotPolicy and SlotType metadata exist (child task 1), integrate validation into the recommendation pipeline so policy-violating decks are never surfaced to the user.

## Integration points

### recommend.GenerateDeck
- After assembling a candidate deck, call DeckSlotPolicy.Validate(deck)
- If validation fails, skip this candidate (don't add to results)
- Policy should be instantiated once per call (1 evo + 1 champ + 1 flex = 3 total)

### Archetype ranking
- internal/recommend/archetype.go or equivalent
- When scoring archetypes, down-rank or discard any archetype whose canonical deck violates the slot policy
- Don't silently swap cards — surface the violation in the archetype's notes/warnings

## What NOT to do
- Do not silently drop evos or champions from a deck to make it valid
- Do not modify the policy constants — they come from slot_policy.go (child task 1)
- Do not break existing tests; update test fixtures to use valid slot assignments

## Files to edit
- internal/recommend/recommend.go (or wherever GenerateDeck lives)
- internal/recommend/archetype.go (or equivalent ranking logic)
- Relevant test files
acceptance_criteria: Policy-violating decks (2+ champions, Balloon evo in regular evo slot) are never returned by GenerateDeck; archetype ranking down-ranks or discards slot-policy-violating archetypes; violations surfaced in notes/warnings (not silently dropped); existing tests pass with slot-valid fixtures
close_reason: Implemented in pkg/recommend/slot_policy.go, slot_metadata.go, slot_combinator.go; wired into recommender.go

title: Add SlotType metadata to cards: RegularEvo, ChampionSlotOnlyEvo, Champion
description:
Create per-card SlotType metadata and a DeckSlotPolicy struct that validates a deck against game rules.

## Card classification (three types)
- **RegularEvo**: standard evolved card (e.g., Skeleton Army evo, Witch evo, Goblin Barrel evo)
  - Can occupy: evo slot OR flex slot
- **ChampionSlotOnlyEvo**: evolved card that only occupies the champion or flex slot (e.g., Balloon evo)
  - Can occupy: champion slot OR flex slot — NEVER the regular evo slot
- **Champion**: dedicated champion card (e.g., Miner, Archer Queen, Bowler-as-champion)
  - Can occupy: champion slot OR flex slot

## Important distinction being captured (reason this task exists)
Bowler has both a regular version (spell/tank) and appears as a champion. Must NOT conflate the two.
Balloon evo is NOT a regular evo — it occupies champion/flex slot only.

## DeckSlotPolicy struct to define
```go
type SlotType int
const (
    RegularCard SlotType = iota
    RegularEvo
    ChampionSlotOnlyEvo
    Champion
)

type DeckSlotPolicy struct {
    MaxEvoSlots     int // = 1 (regular evo slot)
    MaxChampionSlots int // = 1 (champion slot)
    MaxFlexSlots    int // = 1 (flex: evo or champ)
}

func (p DeckSlotPolicy) Validate(deck []CardWithSlotType) error
```

## Files to create/edit
- New file: internal/recommend/slot_policy.go (policy struct + Validate)
- New file: internal/recommend/slot_metadata.go (per-card SlotType table, initially hand-curated)
- Tests: internal/recommend/slot_policy_test.go
acceptance_criteria: SlotType enum with 4 values (RegularCard, RegularEvo, ChampionSlotOnlyEvo, Champion) defined in slot_policy.go; DeckSlotPolicy.Validate() returns error for slot-rule violations; Balloon evo classified as ChampionSlotOnlyEvo; Bowler champion distinguishable from regular Bowler; unit tests in slot_policy_test.go cover all slot types and Validate()
close_reason: Implemented in pkg/recommend/slot_policy.go, slot_metadata.go, slot_combinator.go; wired into recommender.go

title: Model deck evo/champion slot policy and best-assignment combinator
description:
Clash Royale decks have exactly 3 special card slots, but the current codebase has ZERO modeling of this constraint.

## Slot Rules (game mechanics)

- **Evo slot** (1 max): regular evolved cards only — NOT for champion-slot-only evos
- **Champion slot** (1 max): champion cards OR champion-slot-only evos (e.g., Balloon evo)
- **Flex slot** (1 max): either a regular evo or a champion
- **Total special cards**: max 3 (some combination of evos + champions, subject to the above)

Examples of valid assignments:
  - Balloon-evo (champion slot) + Skel-Army-evo (evo slot) + Witch-evo (flex slot) → 2 evos + 0 champs + Balloon in champ slot
  - Balloon-evo (champion slot) + Miner (flex as champion) + Skel-Army-evo (evo slot) → 2 evos + 1 champ

## Known card-type metadata (must be captured)

- Balloon: champion-slot-only evo — cannot go in the regular evo slot
- All standard champions (Miner, Archer Queen, etc.): champion slot or flex slot

## What needs to be built

1. **DeckSlotPolicy struct** with Validate(deck) — model the constraint at deck level; include per-card SlotType metadata (RegularEvo / ChampionSlotOnlyEvo / Champion)
2. **Filter integration** — wire Validate() into recommend.GenerateDeck and archetype ranking so policy-violating decks are rejected
3. **Assignment combinator** — given candidate evos and champions in a deck, enumerate all valid (EvoSlot, FlexSlot, ChampionSlot) assignments, score each via the evaluator, return the top-ranked assignment for display

## Success Criteria
- SlotType metadata implemented for all current CR cards (task `she`)
- DeckSlotPolicy.Validate() wired into recommend.GenerateDeck and archetype ranking (task `77j`)
- Slot assignment combinator enumerates and scores all valid (evo, evo, champion) assignments (task `sl2`)
- No generated deck violates the 3-slot constraint (1 evo + 1 champ + 1 flex)
- Balloon evo and standard champions always placed in game-legal slots in all outputs
notes:
## Success Criteria
- SlotType metadata implemented for all current CR cards (she done)
- DeckSlotPolicy.Validate() wired into GenerateDeck and archetype ranking (77j)
- Slot assignment combinator enumerates and scores all valid (evo, evo, champion) assignments (sl2)
- No generated deck violates the 3-slot constraint
- Balloon evo and standard champions always placed in game-legal slots in all outputs
close_reason: All subtasks (she, 77j, sl2) completed and merged to main

title: Fix deck builder: enforce 1-champion limit and model evolution/champion slot tradeoffs
description:
Two related correctness issues in the deck builder around CR's special slot system:

## Bug: No 1-champion-per-deck enforcement
The builder currently emits decks with 2-3 Champions (e.g. Golden Knight + Skeleton King + Mighty Miner in the same AGGRO deck). Clash Royale only allows 1 Champion per deck. There is zero enforcement anywhere in the builder or generator.

Fix: add a championCount constraint to pkg/deck/builder.go fillRemainingSlots / role selection — once 1 Champion rarity card is in the deck, exclude all other Champion-rarity cards from consideration.

## Feature gap: Champion vs Evolution slot tradeoff not modeled
CR's slot system has distinct constraints for Champions and Evolutions:
- 1 Champion slot (max 1 champion per deck)
- Up to 2 Evolution slots (evolvable cards, scored via EvolutionScorer)
- A flex slot that can hold either an additional Evolution OR counts toward the champion slot

The builder currently treats these as fully independent: evolutionSlotLimit=2 and champion is just a rarity multiplier. The strategic tradeoff of 'should this flex slot be a Champion (ability value) or an evolved card (evolution bonus)?' is never evaluated.

Fix: model the slot system explicitly — track filled champion slots and evolution slots separately during deck construction, evaluate both options for flex slot, and score the tradeoff using the champion ability metadata (see clash-royale-api-ite) vs evolution bonus.

Key files:
- pkg/deck/builder.go: fillRemainingSlots(), selectEvolutionSlots(), scoreCard()
- pkg/deck/types.go: CardCandidate struct may need IsChampion bool
- internal/config/rarity.go: champion card list already exists here

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run `cr-api deck recommend --tag <player-tag>` for any player
2. Inspect the generated decks in the output
3. Observe that multiple Champion-rarity cards (Golden Knight, Skeleton King, Mighty Miner, etc.) can appear in the same deck
4. Confirm against Clash Royale rules: only 1 Champion is allowed per deck
acceptance_criteria: No generated deck contains more than 1 Champion-rarity card; evolution vs champion slot tradeoff is visible in --verbose scoring output; existing suite tests pass
close_reason: Added championLimit (default 1) to Builder; enforced in fillRemainingSlots and post-build enforcement; excess champions replaced with non-champion candidates; RarityChampion constant extracted

title: Add 'cr-api review --tag' orchestrator command
description: A full player review currently requires running 11+ separate sub-commands (player, playstyle, analyze, upgrade-impact, archetypes detect, deck recommend, deck build, deck evaluate, evolutions recommend, deck mulligan, what-if). Add a top-level 'cr-api review --tag <tag>' that bundles: profile snapshot + playstyle summary + top archetype from 'archetypes detect' + top 3 upgrade priorities (cross-archetype impact) + current-deck-vs-best-recommended-deck delta (score, level fit, archetype) + budget-aware 'next 20k gold' suggestion. Output should support --format=human|json|markdown. Lands in the same cr-api binary, not a separate sub-binary. Reuses existing analyzers; this is an orchestrator, not new analysis logic.
close_reason: review orchestrator fully shipped; all children (C1-C4) resolved

title: Fix deck mulligan matchup Key Cards always empty
description: Found while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV. Every matchup block in 'cr-api deck mulligan' output prints 'Key Cards: ' with no entries — affects all 6 matchups (vs Beatdown, Hog Cycle, Bridge Spam, Siege, Control/Bait, Unknown). The matchup struct likely has a KeyCards slice that's never assigned in the matchup constructor. Repro: ./bin/cr-api deck mulligan --cards 'Witch,Golden Knight,Balloon,Dark Prince,Skeleton Dragons,Minion Horde,Bowler,Tornado'
close_reason: Bridge mulligan card-info lookup to internal/config.GetCardRole when local DB misses; treat support troops as defensive (their real-world mulligan function); add KeyCards fallback so empty buckets cascade to plausible alternatives. All 6 matchups now populated. Regression tests added.

title: Fix deck recommend synergy column always reporting 0.0%
description: Found while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV. 'cr-api deck recommend --tag R8QGUQRCV --count 3' reports 'Synergy: 0.0%' for every deck (Deck #1, #2, and #3 all show 0.0%) while 'Compatibility' and 'Overall' have real values. By contrast, 'deck evaluate' produces a real synergy matrix with named pairs. Either synergy isn't being computed in the recommendation pipeline or it isn't attached to the result struct. Trace the synergy scorer used in deck evaluate and check whether the recommendation pipeline calls it or defaults to 0. Repro: ./bin/cr-api deck recommend --tag R8QGUQRCV --count 3
close_reason: Replaced harsh totalScore/28 normalization with avg-strength + coverage blend, mirroring deck evaluate's ScoreSynergy. Added regression tests. Verified end-to-end: previously-0%% archetype decks now report 25-65%%.

title: Fix playstyle command empty Deck/Win Condition/Strategy fields in recommended deck output
description: Found while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV. The 'RECOMMENDED DECK' section in 'cr-api playstyle --recommend-decks' renders 'Deck:', 'Win Condition:', and 'Strategy:' with empty values, even though the deck card list and Match Score render correctly. The struct populates the cards slice but leaves the surrounding metadata blank. Also affects 'OTHER DECK OPTIONS'. Repro: ./bin/cr-api playstyle --tag R8QGUQRCV --recommend-decks. Look in the playstyle command's output templating and the recommended-deck struct construction.
close_reason: Tolerant deck-file decoding + metadata synthesis from deck_detail roles. Tests added in pkg/analysis/deck_recommender_test.go.

title: Combinator: enumerate and score all valid (evo, evo, champion) slot assignments
description:
Given a candidate deck that contains K evo-eligible cards and J champion-eligible cards, enumerate every valid slot assignment and score each to surface the best combination for display.

## Why this matters
For ZyLogan's deck: Balloon-evo (champion-slot-only), Skeleton Army-evo, Witch-evo, and potentially Miner (champion) are all candidates.
The combinator must output which specific assignment (e.g., 'Skel-Army in evo slot, Balloon in champion slot, Witch in flex slot') yields the highest evaluation score.

## Algorithm
1. Collect eligible cards from deck:
   - RegularEvo candidates
   - ChampionSlotOnlyEvo candidates  
   - Champion candidates
2. Enumerate all valid assignments respecting DeckSlotPolicy rules (child task 1)
3. For each assignment, call the evaluator with the fully-specified deck
4. Return top-N assignments sorted by score

## Output struct
```go
type SlotAssignment struct {
    EvoSlot     *CardWithSlotType  // nil if unused
    ChampionSlot *CardWithSlotType // nil if unused
    FlexSlot    *CardWithSlotType  // nil if unused
    Score       float64
}
```

## Files to create/edit
- New file: internal/recommend/slot_combinator.go
- Wire into cr-api review output: show top assignment in DeckDelta or a new section
- Tests: internal/recommend/slot_combinator_test.go

## Acceptance criteria
- Balloon-evo never appears in evo slot in any output
- Bowler-champion and Bowler-regular-card produce different SlotType values
- Top assignment for ZyLogan surfaces Balloon in champion/flex and Skel-Army in evo slot
close_reason: DefaultSlotScorer added to pkg/recommend, EnumerateAssignments wired into cr-api review output as new Slot Assignment section (human + markdown + JSON). ZyLogan acceptance test added.

Implement renderReviewMarkdown(report) modeled on compare_format_markdown.go:12 formatComparisonMarkdown; section helpers write into *strings.Builder. Replace C2 format stubs with real dispatch in review_commands.go.

Get best deck via recommend.NewRecommender(dataDir).GenerateRecommendations(tag, name, result.DeckCardAnalysis) -> Recommendations[0]. Get current deck from result.Player.CurrentDeck. Convert both to []deck.CardCandidate, call C1 DiffDecks. Add DeckDelta section to reviewReport struct and renderReviewHuman renderer.
close_reason: C3 deck delta shipped in review_runtime.go / review_format.go (commit 6fce853)

title: C2: review scaffold + reuse sections + human output
description: New files: cmd/cr-api/review_commands.go (flags+Action), review_runtime.go (logic), review_format.go (rendering). Register addReviewCommand() in main.go Commands slice. Flags: playerTagFlag(true) + validated --format (human|json|markdown, default human). Runtime: call loadOnlinePlayerAnalysis then assemble reviewReport view-model with sections: Profile (Name/Tag/Trophies/ExpLevel/Arena/WL), Playstyle (analysis.AnalyzePlaystyle), Top archetype + cross-arch upgrades (NewDynamicArchetypeDetector, DetectionOptions{TopUpgradesPerArch:3, TopCrossArchUpgrades:10}), Budget next-20k-gold (budget.NewFinder with MaxGoldNeeded:20000, SortByROI). Human renderer renderReviewHuman in review_format.go using output.go helpers. JSON/markdown stubs returning 'not yet implemented'. Shippable standalone; deck-delta section omitted until C3.
close_reason: Implemented review scaffold in cmd/cr-api: review_commands.go (flags+Action using shared constants), review_runtime.go (loadOnlinePlayerAnalysis + archetype detect + budget), review_format.go (human renderer split into 5 helpers + JSON stub + markdown stub). Registered addReviewCommand() in main.go. All lint + tests pass.

title: C1: Shared deck-diff helper (DiffDecks)
description: New pkg/deck/evaluation/deckdiff.go implementing DiffDecks(player, currentCards, recommendedCards []deck.CardCandidate, synergyDB) (*DeckDiff, error). Builds PlayerContext via NewPlayerContextFromPlayer, runs evaluation.Evaluate on both decks, diffs OverallScore, six CategoryScores, DetectedArchetype, and level-fit. DeckDiff fields: CurrentScore, RecommendedScore, ScoreDelta, CategoryDeltas, CurrentArchetype, RecommendedArchetype, CurrentLevelFit, RecommendedLevelFit, SharedCards, ReplacedCards. Includes unit tests for delta math and shared/replaced card sets. Blocks C3 (review delta wiring) and whi.
close_reason: Implemented DiffDecks helper in pkg/deck/evaluation/deckdiff.go with 4 unit tests covering delta math, shared/replaced card sets, zero-delta for identical decks, and level-fit presence.

title: Add champion ability metadata to deck scoring
description:
Champions are scored solely by rarity weight (1.2x multiplier) but their unique abilities are not modeled, causing systematic mis-scoring:

- Golden Knight: dash resets Inferno Tower/Dragon charge — enormous defensive utility not captured
- Mighty Miner: tunnel ignores ground troops mid-push — offensive utility undervalued
- Skeleton King: soul collection grants self-heal and troop summoning — sustain not modeled
- Archer Queen: invisibility cloak dodges spells — defensive survivability not captured
- Little Prince: guard + ranged — combo potential not modeled
- Monk: reflect ability — spell mitigation not captured

Fix: add a hardcoded champion ability metadata table (pkg/config or pkg/deck/role_classifier.go) keyed by card name. Each entry should define: ability type (reset/tunnel/sustain/evasion), defensive value bonus, offensive value bonus, and any synergy tags (e.g. Golden Knight + Inferno Tower counter = high). Factor ability bonuses into scoreCard() alongside the existing rarity boost.

The CR API exposes no ability stats so this must be static metadata. Values should be tunable constants, not magic numbers.
acceptance_criteria: Golden Knight scores measurably higher in decks where Inferno Tower is a known threat archetype; champion ability bonuses are visible in --verbose output; all existing tests pass
close_reason: Added champion ability metadata table (6 abilities), ChampionAbilityBonus constant (0.08), wired into both scoring paths (scoreCard + scoreCardWithEvolutionInternal), with unit test

title: Auto-detect unlocked evolutions from API card data
description:
The deck builder requires --unlocked-evolutions flag (or UNLOCKED_EVOLUTIONS env var) to apply evolution scoring bonuses, but the CR API already returns evolutionLevel per card in the player's collection. This means evolution bonuses are silently zero unless manually declared.

Fix: when building CardCandidate from analysis data, infer unlocked evolutions from cards where EvolutionLevel > 0 or MaxEvolutionLevel > 0. Populate b.unlockedEvolutions automatically before scoreCard is called.

Key files:
- pkg/deck/builder.go: buildCandidate() at line ~440 — add auto-population of b.unlockedEvolutions from data.EvolutionLevel
- pkg/analysis/card_analyzer.go: lines ~101-125 — EvolutionLevel/MaxEvolutionLevel already flow through UpgradeInfo
- cmd/cr-api/deck_builder_config_runtime.go: configureEvolutions() — flag override should still win over auto-detection

The --unlocked-evolutions flag should remain but become an override rather than the only path. Auto-detection should kick in when the flag is absent and API data has EvolutionLevel > 0.
acceptance_criteria: deck build without --unlocked-evolutions flag correctly scores and labels evolution cards when API data shows EvolutionLevel > 0; flag still overrides auto-detection; existing tests pass
close_reason: Auto-detect unlocked evolutions from API EvolutionLevel > 0 data in BuildDeckFromAnalysis when not explicitly configured via flag or env var

title: Extract shared player-analysis mapping helper for cmd runtime commands
description: Triage found repeated mapping from loadOnlinePlayerAnalysis result to command-local structs (player name/tag + deck card analysis) across deck_builder_config_runtime and other cmd runtime handlers. Introduce a shared helper in cmd/cr-api runtime helpers to reduce drift and remove wrapper-level duplication comments. Keep existing typed error context behavior.

title: Decompose discover_commands.go into focused runtime modules
description: cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go is ~1000 lines and mixes flag wiring, process lifecycle management, checkpoint resume logic, and argument synthesis for background execution. Extract cohesive units (flag builders, process control, checkpoint/resume, output formatting) into separate files with narrow helpers. Goal: reduce per-file complexity and make discover command behavior easier to test without full command invocation.

title: Consolidate cmd JSON save paths onto storage.WriteJSON
description: Triage found repeated json.MarshalIndent + os.WriteFile patterns across cmd/cr-api runtime handlers (archetype, budget, whatif, upgrade_impact, mulligan, deck eval batch, leaderboard, compare). We already switched archetype and budget save flows to internal/storage.WriteJSON. Follow up by migrating remaining command save paths and normalizing error messages (include path + operation). Add targeted tests for one representative command to lock behavior.
close_reason: Migrated saveUpgradeImpactAnalysis, saveMulliganGuide, saveWhatIfScenario, and leaderboardExportCommand to storage.WriteJSON; removed redundant MkdirAll+MarshalIndent+WriteFile patterns; existing TestSaveMulliganGuideSanitizesDeckName test verifies behavior

title: Extract shared deck-hash migration helpers for storage packages
description: Triage found near-duplicate deck-hash migration code paths across pkg/leaderboard/storage.go and pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go (row scan, canonical winner selection, duplicate delete/update loop, tie-breaker by score/id). Extract shared helper(s) in pkg/storageutil or new internal package to reduce drift and simplify future hash-migration changes. Keep package-specific behavior hooks (table name, invalid JSON handling policy, post-migration leaderboard stats recalc). Add regression tests in both packages to preserve current semantics.
close_reason: Implemented shared deck-hash migration helpers in storageutil and adopted in leaderboard/fuzzstorage; targeted tests passing

title: Extract shared sqlite deck-storage primitives across leaderboard and fuzzstorage
description: Triage 2026-05-17 found substantial duplication between pkg/leaderboard/storage.go and pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go: both implement near-identical deck-hash migration lifecycle (migrations table check/load rows/winner selection/update-delete), deck hash canonicalization via pkg/deckhash, and duplicate-row cleanup logic with similar tests. Create an internal shared package (e.g. pkg/storageutil/deckstore) for reusable migration helpers and canonical deck row operations while preserving package-specific schema fields (leaderboard stats/player_tag vs fuzz run_id). Add regression tests proving no behavior change for both storage backends.
close_reason: Extracted shared migration lifecycle + winner selection helpers into internal/storageutil and validated leaderboard/fuzzstorage regression tests

title: Consolidate API token validation onto api_helpers across cmd runtime
description: Triage (2026-05-15) found remaining token-validation duplication despite shared helpers in cmd/cr-api/api_helpers.go. Local wrappers still exist in cmd/cr-api/export_commands.go (validateAPIToken) and cmd/cr-api/war_deck.go (inline required token check), with slightly divergent error text from requireAPITokenValue/apiClientOptions. Refactor these callsites to use shared helpers so token resolution + missing-token messaging are centralized and behavior stays consistent across commands. Add focused regression tests for missing-token error pathways in export/war commands.
close_reason: Completed: war command now uses shared requireAPIToken helper; validated missing-token behavior

title: Auto-populate --arena/--league from player profile in deck recommend
description: 'deck recommend' accepts --arena and --league flags but doesn't auto-populate them from the fetched player profile when --tag is provided. For player #R8QGUQRCV the profile reported 'Boot Camp / (empty league)' which suggests post-season-reset state isn't being normalized either. Behavior should be: when --tag is provided and --arena/--league are unset, derive them from the player profile; if league is empty (post-reset), fall back to the player's best-trophy bracket. Explicit flag values still override.
close_reason: deck recommend now derives --arena from player.Arena.Name and --league from player.League.Name when not explicitly set; empty league falls back to best-trophies bracket label. Tests cover all 3 paths.

title: Add 'current deck vs recommended' diff output to playstyle and deck recommend
description: Found while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV. 'playstyle --recommend-decks' shows the player's current deck (Bowler Beatdown) and recommends a different deck (Miner/Graveyard) but doesn't quantify why the alternative is better — no score delta, level-fit delta, archetype-strength delta. Today this requires running deck evaluate twice and comparing by eye. Add a side-by-side diff section showing: overall score current vs recommended, attack/defense/synergy/ladder breakdown deltas, shared cards, replaced cards with reasoning, and gold cost to reach the recommendation. Same enhancement applies to 'deck recommend' when --tag is provided.

title: Add --goal flag to recommendation commands (climb/cheap/cw2/f2p/current-archetype)
description: Every recommendation engine (deck recommend, deck build, analyze, upgrade-impact) currently optimizes a generic score. Real users have explicit goals that should reweight the output: 'climb trophies this season', 'minimum gold to reach competitive', 'Clan War 2 prep (4 decks no repeats)', 'F2P-only path', 'stay on my current archetype'. Add a shared --goal={climb,cheap,cw2,f2p,current-archetype} flag across the recommendation commands. Each goal routes weights differently: e.g. cheap maximizes value/1k-gold, cw2 calls deck war and enforces no card repeats across 4 decks, current-archetype constrains to the player's detected playstyle. Discovered while reviewing player #R8QGUQRCV — the tool gave Royal Giant Cycle as best but couldn't tell us whether that fits a 'spend least gold' goal or a 'climb fastest' goal.

title: Replace events deck hash with shared deckhash utility
description:
pkg/events/deck_matchup.go defines a local deckHash/normalizeDeckNames hashing path (SHA256 + truncated 12-char output) that duplicates canonicalization/hash concerns already centralized in pkg/deckhash. This drift risk is high because normalization rules can diverge silently from leaderboard/fuzz storage dedup behavior. Refactor events hash generation to use a shared helper from pkg/deckhash (or a small wrapper there for truncated IDs), and keep one normalization contract.

Files:
- pkg/events/deck_matchup.go
- pkg/deckhash/hash.go
- pkg/events/parser_test.go (and related tests)

Acceptance:
1) No local SHA256/join implementation remains in events package for deck IDs.
2) Event deck hash behavior is covered by tests for normalization/collision expectations.
3) Hashing contract is documented once in pkg/deckhash.
close_reason: Implemented via PR #87 (shared deckhash helper + events reuse)

title: Unify API token resolution across cmd runtime commands

Affected callsites include cmd/cr-api/fuzz_commands.go, cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go, cmd/cr-api/deck_research_eval_runtime.go, and cmd/cr-api/deck_eval_batch_runtime.go. Refactor to use one token-resolution helper with consistent offline hints and error text.

Create shared builders for discovery flags and forwarded args so defaults/validation live in one place. Include regression tests for CLI parity between start/run/background paths.

title: Extract shared compare-output section renderers
description:
compare_format_table.go, compare_format_markdown.go, and compare_format_report.go repeat the same conceptual rendering loops for best-in-category lines, deck card grids, and verbose defense/attack/synergy sections. This duplication increases drift risk whenever evaluation fields or wording changes.

Candidate extraction points:
- cmd/cr-api/compare_format_table.go:89-103,121-159
- cmd/cr-api/compare_format_markdown.go:69-117
- cmd/cr-api/compare_format_report.go:128-213

Refactor target: shared section data builders + small format-specific emitters so only style differs by output format.
notes: 2026-04-23 triage: current main (f36d74a) already has shared compare formatter helpers and no dupl/gocognit/gocyclo/funlen findings in cmd/cr-api. Re-verify issue scope and close if no remaining formatter duplication risk.
close_reason: Verified resolved on current main (f36d74a): shared compare formatter helpers merged via PR #84; no remaining duplication findings in focused lint triage.

title: Consolidate CSV exporter type-assertion and write boilerplate
description:
CSV export functions repeat the same pattern: type assert input, build rows, instantiate BaseExporter with literal filename, and write to subdir. The repetition makes it easy to diverge on error handling and output paths.

Repeated locations include:
- internal/exporter/csv/player.go:68-79,172-204
- internal/exporter/csv/battle.go:45-56,135-155
- internal/exporter/csv/events.go:25-41,73-113

Refactor target: helper utilities for typed export execution + shared file writer wiring while preserving existing headers and row shapes.

Add shared helpers in cmd/cr-api/deck_build_runtime.go to convert suite deck records into SuiteDeckSummary to reduce duplication and drift while preserving output behavior.

Extract shared orchestration helpers for build loop + suite summary generation to reduce drift and complexity.

title: Deduplicate event command API token validation helper
description: events list/analyze/compare/deck-stats each repeat the same requireAPITokenValue(apiToken, apiClientOptions{}) preflight. Extract shared helper in cmd/cr-api/event_commands.go and add focused tests for required-token error behavior. No behavior change.
close_reason: Extracted shared requireEventAPIToken helper and added tests

title: Calibrate synergy baseline for archetypes with sparse curated DB coverage
description: After clash-royale-api-yde fixed the 0%-floor synergy bug, archetypes whose recommended decks share no curated synergy pairs (cycle, beatdown, control, midrange for some collections) now score the 25%% baseline while bait/siege/spawndeck/bridge_spam score 60-70%% from real DB hits. With the OverallScore weight of 0.25 on synergy, that creates a ~10-point ranking gap between 'documented' and 'undocumented' archetypes that's driven by curated DB coverage rather than actual deck quality. Two related follow-ups: (a) decide whether 25%% is the right baseline (compare top recommendations against external meta data for a few real player tags), and (b) expand the curated synergy DB (pkg/deck/synergy.go NewSynergyDatabase) to cover cycle/beatdown core pairs so coverage isn't archetype-biased. Reproduce: ./bin/cr-api deck recommend --tag R8QGUQRCV --count 8 --from-analysis.

title: Audit and update stale clash-royale-api refs in docs/ and pkg/
description: Follow-up to clash-royale-api-bs1: the new scripts/check-stale-beads-refs.sh surfaces 9 additional stale ID refs (33f5, 38h, 4nga, 5xb, ao8, bwq8, e6m, gv5, hj9j) outside the cmd/cr-api/ surface that bs1 covered. Most live in research/design docs (docs/IMPROVED_SCORING_DESIGN.md, docs/INTEGRATION_DESIGN.md, docs/REDUNDANCY_PATTERNS.md, docs/VERSATILITY_METRICS.md, docs/LEVEL_CURVES_IMPLEMENTATION.md, docs/COUNTER_RELATIONSHIPS.md, docs/DECK_SYNERGIES.md, docs/CODE_QUALITY_*.md) plus a few code refs (pkg/deck/scorer_v2.go, pkg/deck/redundancy_scorer.go, pkg/deck/versatility_scorer.go, pkg/deck/genetic/genome_test.go). For most of these, the original beads ID was a research/design task that's been closed and never replaced; rewrite each ref as 'closed: clash-royale-api-XXXX (superseded)' or replace with the active follow-up issue. Verify with: task lint-beads-refs --strict

title: Reconcile project beads docs with CLI schema: 'docs' is not a valid issue type
description:
Hit while filing issues from the #R8QGUQRCV cr-api review on 2026-05-05. The project's CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md documents 'docs' as a valid issue type ('Types: task, bug, feature, epic, question, docs' in one section, and a separate 'Issue Types' table that includes 'chore'). The bd CLI rejects --type=docs with 'validation failed for issue : invalid issue type: docs'. Empirically valid types in this workspace appear to be: bug, feature, task, epic, chore, question.

Fix one of two ways:
1. Update CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to drop 'docs' and use 'task' for documentation work (matches the schema and matches what the original retry succeeded with), OR
2. Add 'docs' to the beads CLI's accepted issue types if there's intent to support it.

Option 1 is the cheap correct fix; option 2 only makes sense if the upstream beads tool wants to support it. Repro:
  BD_DOLT_MODE=embedded bd create --title='X' --description='Y' --type=docs --priority=4
  # → Error: validation failed for issue : invalid issue type: docs
close_reason: Updated CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md to reflect the actual valid bd issue types (chore replacing docs); added explicit note that docs is not accepted.

title: Unify combat-stats flags across analyze, upgrade-impact, deck build
description: The same scoring concept is exposed three different ways: 'upgrade-impact' has --use-combat-stats (boolean opt-in), 'deck build' has --combat-stats-weight (0.0-1.0) and --disable-combat-stats, 'analyze' has neither. This is confusing and the inconsistent surface makes scripting harder. Pick one shape (suggest: --combat-stats-weight float with 0 disabling and a sensible default) and adopt it across all three commands.

title: Battle-log scan for non-event ladder games (per-deck win rate over time)
description: 'events scan' covers challenges/tournaments but there is no equivalent for non-event ladder play. Player #R8QGUQRCV has 1236 battles with 55.5% win rate, but we have no way to ask: which deck did they actually use last week, what is their per-deck win rate over time, are they trending up or down. The official API exposes recent battle log; add a scan that aggregates ladder games by detected deck signature and emits per-deck W/L/3-crown stats and a trajectory chart. Feeds the proposed cr-api review orchestrator with real performance data instead of just snapshot scores.

title: Add bulk shard import for evolutions shards (JSON paste / OCR / assume-N mode)
description: 'evolutions recommend' is built around shard counts but the official Clash Royale API doesn't expose shard inventory, and 'evolutions shards set' requires per-card manual entry. The feature is effectively inert today — for player #R8QGUQRCV every card showed 0/10 shards and the recommendations were advisory-only. Need a bulk-import path: (1) JSON paste/file import, (2) optional OCR from a screenshot of the shard inventory screen, (3) a 'assume-N' mode that fills in N shards/card uniformly so recommendations become directional even without exact data. At minimum (1) and (3) are achievable without API changes.

title: Resolve pre-existing golangci-lint issues in cmd/cr-api/fuzz_commands.go
description:
Surfaced by 'task lint' on 2026-05-04 (branch codex/refactor-shared-local-db-paths). Pre-existing on main; unblocked by the datapath refactor (clash-royale-api-x7h) but not introduced by it.

Findings:
- fuzz_commands.go:1791  errcheck: storage.Count() return error is discarded.
- fuzz_commands.go:1931  funlen: reevaluateStoredDecks is 63 lines (>60).
- fuzz_commands.go:1691  gocognit: deckFuzzListCommand cognitive complexity 30 (>20).
- fuzz_commands.go:1811  gocognit: deckFuzzUpdateCommand cognitive complexity 24 (>20).

Acceptance:
1) 'task lint' exits 0.
2) Behavior of fuzz list/update/reevaluate paths unchanged (covered by existing cmd/cr-api tests).
close_reason: Fixed all 4 originally-flagged issues + 3 newly-surfaced goconst constants. Replaced literal 'json'/'csv'/'detailed' with fuzzOutput* constants; logged storage.Count error in verbose mode; extracted buildFuzzQueryOptions, resolveFuzzWorkers, applyEvaluationToEntry, newReevaluateProgressBar, advanceReevaluateBar, reevaluateForPlayer, dispatchFuzzListFormatter helpers to drop both deckFuzz commands under the gocyclo/funlen/gocognit thresholds. golangci-lint reports 0 issues for the package; all 2517 tests pass.

title: Deduplicate discover state directory path builders
description: discover state/checkpoint directory defaults are duplicated across cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go and pkg/deck/discovery_checkpoint.go despite pkg/deck/discovery_paths.go already centralizing the base path. Refactor to route defaults through deck.DiscoverySessionBaseDir() to reduce drift.
notes: Implemented shared discover state-dir usage in cmd/cr-api/discover_commands.go and pkg/deck/discovery_checkpoint.go; both now call deck.DiscoverySessionBaseDir().
close_reason: Refactor merged into branch codex/refactor-discover-state-dir; tests passed; PR #90 opened.

title: Unify per-user database path construction for local data stores
description:
Per-user storage path assembly is duplicated across cmd and storage packages (e.g., cmd/cr-api/leaderboard_commands.go manual ~/.cr-api/leaderboards path building, pkg/leaderboard/storage.go path composition, and pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go default path composition). Introduce a shared path builder utility for ~/.cr-api roots and store-specific filenames so path rules and sanitization stay consistent.

Files:
- cmd/cr-api/leaderboard_commands.go
- pkg/leaderboard/storage.go
- pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go

Acceptance:
1) Path construction for local db files uses shared helper(s).
2) Player tag sanitization remains delegated to playertag.Sanitize.
3) No behavior change in resulting file locations.
notes: 2026-05-02 automation run: implemented shared local DB path helper in internal/datapath and refactored pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go, pkg/leaderboard/storage.go, and cmd/cr-api/leaderboard_commands.go to use it; added internal/datapath/datapath_test.go; targeted tests passed (go test ./internal/datapath ./pkg/fuzzstorage ./pkg/leaderboard ./cmd/cr-api). Unable to commit/push in /Users/klauer/dev/clash-royale-api because .git/index.lock exists and this environment blocks removing lock files, so PR creation is currently blocked.
close_reason: Shared ~/.cr-api path helpers landed in internal/datapath (commit 899b806); pkg/leaderboard, pkg/fuzzstorage, and cmd/cr-api/leaderboard_commands.go now consume them. Tests pass.

title: Extract shared sqlite close/log helper for storage packages
description:
Both pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go and pkg/leaderboard/storage.go implement identical closeWithLog helpers and near-identical sqlite init scaffolding. Extract a shared internal helper package for safe close logging and common storage setup patterns to reduce duplicated error-handling code and future drift.

Files:
- pkg/fuzzstorage/storage.go
- pkg/leaderboard/storage.go
- new internal helper package (e.g., internal/storageutil)

Acceptance:
1) closeWithLog is defined once and reused by both packages.
2) Existing behavior/log messages are preserved.
3) Existing storage tests remain green.
close_reason: Implemented shared storageutil.CloseWithLog helper; tests passed; PR opened

title: Prune unused phased-refactor structs/helpers in suite/evaluation runtime
description: Multiple runtime files carry nolint:unused markers for deferred refactors (deck_analyze_suite_runtime.go, deck_compare_suite_runtime.go, deck_eval_batch_runtime.go, deck_eval_format_runtime.go, pkg/deck/evaluation/analysis.go). Audit which items are still reachable, remove dead code, and keep only compatibility shims that are externally required.

title: Cross-command score normalization or scoring guide appendix
description: Score scales across cr-api commands aren't comparable: 'playstyle' reports 40/100, 'archetypes detect' reports 76.6 (also /100 but a different scale), 'deck evaluate' reports 5.3/10, 'upgrade-impact' reports 377 (unbounded raw points). Nothing in the CLI explains how to read across these. Two options: (a) normalize all user-facing scores to a single 0-100 with documented bands (Optimal 90+, Competitive 75-89, etc), (b) add a 'cr-api help scoring' or short markdown appendix explaining each scale and how they relate. Option (a) is better; (b) is the cheap fallback.

title: Hide or compute 'Key Cards Found' line in upgrade-impact summary
description: 'cr-api upgrade-impact' reports 'Key Cards Found: 0' in the Summary section even when 10 high-impact upgrades are listed below it. The 'key archetype card' bucket appears to be a separate concept that simply isn't populated for this player. Either (a) compute it correctly (define what makes a card 'key' — top archetype win conditions in the player's most viable archetypes?), or (b) hide the line when the bucket is empty so the summary doesn't show misleading zeros. Discovered on player #R8QGUQRCV.
